details: High-asset divorce client from Ryan Magath (forensic accountant NJ). No money management experience. First lead from referral pipeline.
advisorApproach: Warm intro through Ryan. Focus on wealth protection post-divorce, investment basics, fresh start planning.

triggerEvent: Brickell CPA serving $500K+ entrepreneurs - Act 60 specialist - no advisor partnership
details: Delerme CPA, Brickell. Founded 2009. Act 60 specialist, high-income entrepreneurs, global investors. No existing advisor partnership. linkedin.com/in/victor-delerme/
advisorApproach: Victor, I work with entrepreneurs in Miami who could benefit from our collaboration. Your Act 60 expertise paired with my wealth planning is a natural fit.

triggerEvent: Big 4 CPA 20+ years serving construction and business owners Brickell
details: E. Gomez CPA LLC, Brickell. 20+ years, Big 4 background. Construction contractors, freight/trucking, small business owners.
advisorApproach: Hi Edgar, your Big 4 background and construction/freight client base pairs well with my practice. Many business owners need both tax and wealth planning.

triggerEvent: CPA serving real estate investors startups and HNW Miami
details: Quilca CPA Group. Real estate investors, startups, HNW individuals. FIU grad, 500+ LinkedIn connections, international tax planning.
advisorApproach: Hi Edward, your real estate and startup client base aligns with wealth events I track. Would love to explore a referral partnership.

triggerEvent: Attorney and CPA at 800 Brickell 44 years international HNW clients
details: Brickell Accountants, 800 Brickell Ave. Attorney + CPA. International clients since 1980. FIRPTA and cross-border wealth.
advisorApproach: Hi Alvaro, your 44 years at 800 Brickell serving international HNW clients makes you an ideal partner. Would love a quick coffee.

triggerEvent: IRS resolution CPA clients rebuilding wealth post-crisis
details: Virtual firm. IRS problem resolution, financial modeling, veteran-focused. Modern approach, open to partnerships.
advisorApproach: Your IRS resolution clients are rebuilding wealth after crisis - perfect point to introduce comprehensive financial planning.

triggerEvent: 40-year Aventura dental practice owner nearing retirement succession planning needed
details: Aventura Dental Arts LLC. Senior partner since 1986. Age ~60. Income $500K-1M+. Specialty esthetic/implant practice. No financial advisor mentioned on website.
advisorApproach: Dr. Grillo, you have built Aventura Dental Arts over nearly 40 years. Have you started planning how to transition the practice or diversify wealth beyond the business?

triggerEvent: South Florida event rental company - 40 years - owner retiring - $3.5M revenue
details: Full-service event rental company. 40 years in South Florida market. Owner retiring, transitioned to management role. $3.5M revenue, 37% EBITDA projected. Tents, structures, catering equipment. Loyal corporate/municipal client base. Listed via Synergy Business Brokers.
advisorApproach: Owner of a 40-year business about to receive a significant liquidity event from the sale. Likely has not planned for wealth management post-sale. Approach via business broker or direct outreach.

triggerEvent: South Florida IT cloud services company 30+ years owner selling $1.5M revenue
prospectName: IT Cloud Services Company Owner (South Florida)
details: 30+ year IT/cloud services company. $1.5M TTM revenue, 80% recurring subscriptions. Owner selling. $590K seller discretionary earnings. Microsoft Gold partner. 200 clients.
advisorApproach: Tech business owner about to exit after 30 years. Will receive lump sum. Classic case for tax-efficient deployment, diversification away from concentrated business wealth.

triggerEvent: South Florida water treatment company $3.5M revenue 4 partners 80% Florida market share
details: Water treatment niche company. $3.5M revenue, 37% EBITDA. 4 active operational partners + 1 investor. 80% Florida market control. National competitors cannot compete regionally.
advisorApproach: Four business partners approaching a liquidity event. Each partner will need separate wealth planning. Partnership exit = multiple client opportunities.

triggerEvent: Miami estate attorney 25 years TV appearances JD MBA CFP - rare financial background
details: Frye, Fortich & Garcia P.L., North Miami. 25+ years. JD, MBA, CFP designation - extremely rare for estate attorney. TV appearances on WPBT Nightly Business Report. Serves HNW clients on estate and financial planning.
advisorApproach: Austin, your CFP designation is rare among estate attorneys. I serve clients with the investment/insurance side of what you plan legally. Would love to explore how we can refer to each other.

triggerEvent: Miami solo estate planning probate and guardianship attorney
details: Solo practitioner, Miami. Estate planning, probate, guardianship, complex trust drafting. Independent attorney - not tied to a large firm, likely open to referral partnerships.
advisorApproach: Jacqueline, I work with HNW families on the financial side of estate planning. Your probate and trust work complements my investment and insurance practice. Worth a quick coffee?

triggerEvent: Miami Beach international estate planning attorney inbound and outbound tax planning
details: Bianchi Fasani Law P.A., 1000 5th Street Miami Beach. International tax planning, pre-immigration tax, estate planning, real property. Serves cross-border HNW clients.
advisorApproach: Beatrice, your international client base in Miami Beach is exactly who I work with on the investment and insurance side. Pre-immigration planning clients especially need wealth management.

triggerEvent: Highly reviewed Miami estate planning attorney 20+ reviews solo practice
details: Solo estate planning attorney, Miami/Miami Beach area. 20+ verified Avvo reviews, 5.0 rating. Independent practice. Serves individual clients on wills, trusts, estates.
advisorApproach: Donna, I work with clients on the wealth management side of estate planning. Your highly-rated solo practice is the kind of boutique referral relationship I look for. Worth connecting?

triggerEvent: Miami dental practice owner 40 years key staff retention via VUL executive bonus plan
prospectName: Dr. Larry Grillo - VUL Executive Bonus
details: Aventura Dental Arts LLC. 40-year practice with experienced staff. Owner nearing retirement - key to retaining top hygienists and associates during ownership transition. Section 162 executive bonus plan using VUL is ideal tool.
advisorApproach: Dr. Grillo, as you think about the next chapter, one challenge is keeping your best team members through the transition. A VUL-funded executive bonus plan lets you reward and retain them tax-efficiently. Worth a 20-minute conversation?

triggerEvent: Miami fintech CEO growing company key employee retention via Section 162 VUL
details: MAJORITY fintech CEO, Miami. Company serving U.S. immigrants, growing rapidly. Needs to attract and retain senior tech talent in competitive Miami market. Executive bonus plan via VUL is a differentiator vs. stock options.
advisorApproach: Magnus, in fintech you are competing with FAANG for engineering talent. A Section 162 executive bonus plan using VUL gives you a retention tool that stock options cannot match - portable, tax-advantaged, personally owned by the employee.

triggerEvent: South Florida business owner profitable company key employee golden handcuffs needed
prospectName: South Florida Business Owners - VUL Pipeline
details: Category: Miami-area business owners with 5-50 employees and $500K+ income looking to retain key executives. Classic Section 162 executive bonus plan candidates. Ideal to approach via CPA referral partners (Victor Delerme, Edgar Gomez) who already serve these clients.
advisorApproach: Via CPA partners: Many of your business owner clients have retention challenges. A VUL executive bonus plan is a simple, tax-deductible way to create golden handcuffs for key employees. I can present to any client you think is a fit.
